entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop species Pennisetum americanum studied for spacing variation

entities.name: PLANT SPACING VARIANCE
entities.description: The variance of spacing (Vs) between plants in a row

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: PLANT POPULATION
entities.description: The number of plants per unit area

entities.name: FERTILITY
entities.description: The nutrient level or fertility of the soil/cropping situation

entities.name: SEASON
entities.description: The time of year or specific growing season

entities.name: PANICLE NUMBER
entities.description: The number of panicles produced per plant

entities.name: TILLERING CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of the plant to produce tillers to adjust to population changes

relationships.description: Increase in spacing variance leads to a decline in grain yield
Reduction in grain yield due to spacing variance is attributed to a reduction in panicle number per plant
Yield reduction caused by spacing variance does not depend on fertility level
Yield reduction caused by spacing variance does not depend on the season
Yield reduction caused by spacing variance does not depend on plant population
Tillering capacity provides the ability to adjust to a wide range of plant populations
Tillering capacity does not provide adjustment to major changes in spacing variance
The crop whose grain yield is measured in the study
The crop that possesses tillering capacity
